task: Competitive research: AI agents + ternary computing 2025-2026
steps: Researched 7 AI agent orchestrators (Kiro, Antigravity, Codex, Cursor, Claude Code, Devin, CrewAI)
Researched spec-first development tools (Kiro EARS, Red Hat spec coding)
Researched ternary/HDC papers (BitNet b1.58, TENET, Tequila, Sherry, DecoHD)
Researched self-improving agents (Reflexion, ADAS, AgentTrek, ExpeL, Self-Refine)
learnings: Kiro uses markdown EARS specs — Trinity .tri DSL is more powerful but less known
BitNet b1.58 is the key ternary benchmark — 2B params, {-1,0,+1}, matches full-precision
TENET Sparse Ternary LUT: 4.3x energy efficiency on FPGA — directly applicable to hslm_ternary_mac.v
Tequila fixes dead weights at deadzone boundary — may improve HSLM convergence
DecoHD decomposed encoding: 277x energy gains — could reduce FPGA BRAM from 95%
Error cascading is primary failure mode — per-link Golden Chain checkpoints address this
AgentTrek: generate specs from successful runs — experience-to-spec pipeline
No competitor does spec round-tripping (code drift → spec update) — first-mover opportunity
strategic_actions: Publish .tri spec format as open standard
Benchmark HSLM vs BitNet b1.58 with honest numbers
Study TENET for FPGA pipeline optimization
